teachingPoint: Shape heuristics enable intelligent type inference when Miro shape element lacks explicit type hint. Heuristics: if aspectRatio > 2.0 then LINE, if aspectRatio < 0.3 then COLUMN, if height > width then VERTICAL_BAR, else GENERIC_SHAPE. Heuristic confidence score stored with shape record.
positiveExample: T658 ShapeHeuristicAnalyzer reads aspectRatio from shape.bounds. If 2.5: classified as LINE with confidence=0.92. If 0.25: classified as COLUMN with confidence=0.88. Heuristic applied only if explicit type missing.
negativeExample: Unknown shapes silently treated as GENERIC_SHAPE without heuristic analysis. No confidence score recorded. Different inferences on re-process due to lack of deterministic logic.
discriminatingConstraint: Shape heuristics applied only when explicit type missing. Confidence score recorded in shape record. Heuristics deterministic and reproducible (same input → same inference + score). ORDER 1 heuristic calculation.

teachingPoint: Error handling for unsupported element types: Miro may return shape types not in XIIGen canonicalization map. Unknown types must be logged with full shape metadata, stored in xiigen-unsupported-elements for audit, and return graceful failure without blocking flow.
positiveExample: T658 receives shapeType='CUSTOM_CONNECTOR'. Not in mapping. Stores record in xiigen-unsupported-elements with { originalType: 'CUSTOM_CONNECTOR', boardId, shapeId, metadata, discoveredAt }. Returns ShapeTypeNotSupported failure code.
negativeExample: Unknown shape type causes exception. Entire flow blocked. No audit trail of unsupported type. Error message unhelpful for debugging.
discriminatingConstraint: Unknown types stored in xiigen-unsupported-elements for audit. Graceful failure returns ShapeTypeNotSupported without exception. Complete metadata logged for triage.

teachingPoint: Tenant isolation in adapter context: boardId must belong to current tenant. Miro boards are workspace-scoped; APIKey in factory confirms workspace match. Before processing any shape on board, verify boardId tenant ownership via fabric, not request parameter.
positiveExample: T658 ShapeHeuristicAnalyzer calls fabric.getBoardMetadata(boardId). Fabric internally validates APIKey workspace matches boardId. If mismatch: returns UnauthorizedBoardAccess. No shapes processed until board ownership confirmed.
negativeExample: boardId passed in request, trusted directly. No workspace validation. Cross-tenant board access possible if attacker knows boardId from other tenant.
discriminatingConstraint: Board ownership validated at fabric layer (not service logic). APIKey workspace confirmed matches boardId. ORDER 1 before any shape processing.

teachingPoint: Coordinate system translation for Miro boards: Miro uses infinite canvas (negative coordinates allowed). XIIGen normalizes to page-relative coordinates within visible viewport bounds. Transformation: (miroX - boardMinX) / viewportWidth * 100. Viewport bounds from board metadata.
positiveExample: T658 reads board.viewport = { minX: -5000, maxX: 5000, minY: -2000, maxY: 2000 }. For shape at miroX: 0: normalizedX = (0 - (-5000)) / 10000 * 100 = 50. Stores normalized coords in transform record.
negativeExample: Using raw miroX/miroY directly. Layout breaks when viewport changes. No portable representation without full board context.
discriminatingConstraint: Viewport bounds read from board metadata. Coordinate transformation formula applied at ORDER 2. Normalized values 0-100 relative to viewport.

description: Shape heuristics enable intelligent type inference when explicit type hint absent. Aspect-ratio based inference (aspectRatio > 2.0 → LINE, < 0.3 → COLUMN) with confidence scoring. Deterministic and reproducible across reprocessing.

Error handling for unsupported element types. Unknown types stored in xiigen-unsupported-elements index for audit and future support planning. Graceful failure (ShapeTypeNotSupported) prevents flow blocking.
Board ownership validation via fabric layer. APIKey workspace confirmed matches boardId before shape processing. Prevents cross-tenant board access via boardId guessing.

Coordinate translation for infinite canvas (allows negative coords). Normalizes to page-relative coords within viewport bounds. Formula: (miroCoord - boardMin) / viewportSize * 100. Portable across viewport changes.
